base_caption.description: Generates the neutral factual description all four styles build from
base_caption.prompt:
You are given a sequence of frames sampled from a short video clip, in chronological order. Describe what happens in the clip in clear, neutral, factual language.

Cover:
- Who or what is present (people, animals, objects)
- What actions occur and in what order
- The setting or environment
- Any visible on-screen text or graphics

Write 2-4 sentences, in English. Describe the clip as a whole, not frame by frame. Do not add interpretation, opinions, emotion, or speculation about things not visible.

generic_style.description: Fallback template for any requested style that has no dedicated prompt above
generic_style.prompt:
Rewrite the following video description as a caption in a '{style_name}' style and tone. Stay faithful to the described content - do not invent things that are not in the description. Write 1-3 sentences.

Return ONLY the caption text, nothing else.

Video description:
{description}

formal.label: Formal
formal.prompt:
Rewrite the following video description as a formal caption, as if written for an official archive, corporate report, or museum placard.

Voice rules:
- Precise, complete sentences; no contractions, no slang, no exclamation marks
- Objective and measured; prefer exact terms over vague ones
- Third person throughout
- 1-3 sentences, information-dense but not stiff to the point of parody

Return ONLY the caption text, nothing else.

Video description:
{description}

sarcastic.label: Sarcastic
sarcastic.prompt:
Rewrite the following video description as a sarcastic caption, in the voice of a dry, unimpressed commentator who has seen it all.

Voice rules:
- Deadpan irony: praise trivial things as monumental, or treat dramatic things as mundane
- Use rhetorical questions, faux-earnest applause, or weary observations ('Ah yes...', 'Truly groundbreaking...')
- Wit comes from understatement and eye-rolling, NOT from jokes, puns, or tech references
- Never mean-spirited toward real people; the sarcasm targets the situation
- 1-3 sentences

Return ONLY the caption text, nothing else.

Video description:
{description}

humorous_tech.label: Humorous (Tech)
humorous_tech.prompt:
Rewrite the following video description as a funny caption written BY a software engineer FOR other engineers.

Voice rules:
- Frame what happens in the clip using programming and tech metaphors: bugs, deploys, merge conflicts, race conditions, uptime, CPU/GPU, APIs, machine learning, code review
- The humor is affectionate insider humor - the fun comes from mapping everyday events onto tech concepts accurately
- Pick ONE or TWO tech metaphors that genuinely fit the action; do not pile on jargon
- Upbeat, not sarcastic - this voice is enthusiastic, not eye-rolling
- 1-3 sentences

Return ONLY the caption text, nothing else.

Video description:
{description}

humorous_non_tech.label: Humorous (Non-Tech)
humorous_non_tech.prompt:
Rewrite the following video description as a funny caption for a general audience - something that would work as a viral social media caption anyone's grandmother would also laugh at.

Voice rules:
- Everyday humor: wordplay, puns, playful exaggeration, sports-commentary energy, or giving subjects funny motivations and inner monologues
- Absolutely NO technology references, programming terms, or internet-insider jargon
- Warm and playful, never mocking
- 1-3 sentences

Return ONLY the caption text, nothing else.

Video description:
{description}
